violations.text: The cutting boards on the prep units are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Tzatziki sauce and cheese were observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food was placed in the prep unit about 1/2 hour prior to inspection. Food was relocated to the refrigerator at time of inspection. Food was observed stored over the fill line. Establishment must discontinue storing food over the fill line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Chicken was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #2. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food was placed in the prep unit about 1/2 hour prior to inspection. Food was relocated to the refrigerator at time of inspection. Food was observed stored over the fill line. Establishment must discontinue storing food over the fill line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Sauce and deli meats were observed held at 44-47 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #3. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food was placed in the prep unit about 1/2 hour prior to inspection. Food was relocated to the refrigerator at time of inspection. Food was observed stored over the fill line. Establishment must discontinue storing food over the fill line. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cream sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Cream sauce was relocated to shallow pans to cool during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
Knives were observed stored on an unclean surface (between the prep units). In-use utensils must be stored on a clean surface between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The cutting boards in and prep unit cutting blocks are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Pico and tartar sauce was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment shall only be held for 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pomodoro sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Items were moved to shallow pans to properly cool. Cooling methods guide left with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the wood panels above the prep line and the ice machine has an accumulation of dust and biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023
